Index

Rosie Hohnen

2026-03-19

Read in libraries

#----Load libraries and check data----
library(ggpubr)
library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(tidyverse)
library(stats)
library(MuMIn)
library(lme4)
library(glmmTMB)
library(DHARMa)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom.mixed)
library(car)
library(performance)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

Read in data

Also standardize variables and check correlations

# Read in script
Trans_2 <-read.csv('Transloc_sum_V5.csv', header = TRUE)
summary(Trans_2)
##    ReleaseID         Year          Type              State          
##  Min.   : 1.0   Min.   :1999   Length:27          Length:27         
##  1st Qu.: 7.5   1st Qu.:2011   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Median :14.0   Median :2018   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##  Mean   :14.0   Mean   :2015                                        
##  3rd Qu.:20.5   3rd Qu.:2020                                        
##  Max.   :27.0   Max.   :2021                                        
##                                                                     
##     Colour            Rel_loc          Release.Aviary.ID     Total_r     
##  Length:27          Length:27          Length:27          Min.   : 3.00  
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.: 4.50  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Median : 7.00  
##                                                           Mean   :13.44  
##                                                           3rd Qu.:20.00  
##                                                           Max.   :48.00  
##                                                                          
##     Transloc     Prop_Transloc    Season_survive   Prop_Season_survival
##  Min.   : 0.00   Min.   :0.0000   Min.   : 0.000   Min.   :0.0000      
##  1st Qu.: 2.00   1st Qu.:0.3667   1st Qu.: 1.000   1st Qu.:0.1548      
##  Median : 5.00   Median :0.6571   Median : 2.000   Median :0.3333      
##  Mean   :10.04   Mean   :0.6344   Mean   : 4.889   Mean   :0.3161      
##  3rd Qu.:13.50   3rd Qu.:0.9019   3rd Qu.: 7.500   3rd Qu.:0.4115      
##  Max.   :45.00   Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :25.000   Max.   :1.0000      
##                                                                        
##     Returns        Prop_Returns     Total_release    Tranmitters    
##  Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :0.00000   Min.   : 3.00   Min.   : 0.000  
##  1st Qu.:0.0000   1st Qu.:0.00000   1st Qu.: 4.50   1st Qu.: 2.000  
##  Median :0.0000   Median :0.00000   Median : 7.00   Median : 4.000  
##  Mean   :0.4444   Mean   :0.01906   Mean   :13.44   Mean   : 4.407  
##  3rd Qu.:1.0000   3rd Qu.:0.03239   3rd Qu.:20.00   3rd Qu.: 6.000  
##  Max.   :3.0000   Max.   :0.12500   Max.   :48.00   Max.   :14.000  
##                                                                     
##   Prop_transm        Training      Conspecifics_bin  Conspecifics   
##  Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :0.0000  
##  1st Qu.:0.2955   1st Qu.:0.0000   1st Qu.:0.0000   1st Qu.:0.0000  
##  Median :0.8000   Median :0.0000   Median :0.0000   Median :0.0000  
##  Mean   :0.6514   Mean   :0.1481   Mean   :0.1481   Mean   :0.1852  
##  3rd Qu.:1.0000   3rd Qu.:0.0000   3rd Qu.:0.0000   3rd Qu.:0.0000  
##  Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :2.0000  
##                                                                     
##      Aviary        Supp_food     Days_supp_food      Year.1    
##  Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 5.00   Min.   : 0.00   Min.   :1999  
##  1st Qu.: 0.00   1st Qu.: 7.00   1st Qu.: 0.00   1st Qu.:2011  
##  Median : 3.00   Median :13.00   Median : 2.00   Median :2018  
##  Mean   : 5.55   Mean   :17.75   Mean   : 5.35   Mean   :2015  
##  3rd Qu.: 7.00   3rd Qu.:22.00   3rd Qu.: 8.50   3rd Qu.:2020  
##  Max.   :40.00   Max.   :60.00   Max.   :23.00   Max.   :2021  
##  NA's   :7       NA's   :7       NA's   :7                     
##    State.1          Review.info        Release.aviary.ID  Release.date      
##  Length:27          Length:27          Length:27          Length:27         
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##  Release.location  
##  Length:27         
##  Class :character  
##  Mode  :character  
##                    
##                    
##                    
## 
# Standardize key variables
Trans_2 <- Trans_2 %>%
  mutate(Transmitters_std = as.numeric(scale(Tranmitters))) %>%
  mutate(Training_std = as.numeric(scale(Training))) %>%
  mutate(Conspecifics_std = as.numeric(scale(Conspecifics))) %>%
  mutate(Total_release_std = as.numeric(scale(Total_release)))

# Check correlations
correlation_subset <- Trans_2[, c(10,12,14,15,30,31,32,33)]
chart.Correlation(correlation_subset, histogram=TRUE)

Run models on translocation persistence

#----Run models on translocation survival----
f1 <- glm(cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Total_release_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
f2 <- glm(cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Transmitters_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
f3 <- glm(cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Training_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
f4 <- glm(cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Total_release_std + Transmitters_std + Training_std , family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
f5 <- glm(cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ 1 , family="binomial", data=Trans_2)

summary(f4)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Total_release_std + 
##     Transmitters_std + Training_std, family = "binomial", data = Trans_2)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)         0.6590     0.1462   4.507 6.59e-06 ***
## Total_release_std   0.5247     0.1505   3.487 0.000488 ***
## Transmitters_std   -0.1569     0.1501  -1.045 0.295906    
## Training_std       -0.2596     0.1495  -1.737 0.082440 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 106.160  on 26  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  61.184  on 23  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 125.48
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
summary(f1)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = cbind(Transloc, Total_r - Transloc) ~ Total_release_std, 
##     family = "binomial", data = Trans_2)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)         0.5904     0.1410   4.188 2.82e-05 ***
## Total_release_std   0.6978     0.1203   5.798 6.70e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 106.160  on 26  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  68.006  on 25  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 128.31
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

Run model selection

# Run model selection
fmList1<-model.sel(f1=f1, f2=f2, f3=f3, f4=f4, f5=f5)
fmList1
## Model selection table 
##     (Int) Ttl_rls_std Trns_std Trnn_std df  logLik  AICc delta weight
## f4 0.6590      0.5247  -0.1569  -0.2596  4 -58.742 127.3  0.00  0.677
## f1 0.5904      0.6978                    2 -62.153 128.8  1.50  0.319
## f2 0.9201              -0.5695           2 -66.631 137.8 10.46  0.004
## f3 1.0490                       -0.5613  2 -71.904 148.3 21.01  0.000
## f5 1.0800                                1 -81.230 164.6 37.32  0.000
## Models ranked by AICc(x)

Check residuals

Most of the red flags appear to be derived from outliers which is hard to avoid with a small data set. The values of dispersion, uniformity are acceptable.

# Check residuals
sim_f4 <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = f4, n = 1000)
plot(sim_f4)

testDispersion(sim_f4)

## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## dispersion = 1.0198, p-value = 0.758
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
testUniformity(sim_f4)

## 
##  Exact one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
## D = 0.14703, p-value = 0.5544
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
testOutliers(sim_f4)

## 
##  DHARMa bootstrapped outlier test
## 
## data:  sim_f4
## outliers at both margin(s) = 1, observations = 27, p-value = 0.08
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
##  percent confidence interval:
##  0.00000000 0.03703704
## sample estimates:
## outlier frequency (expected: 0.00148148148148148 ) 
##                                         0.03703704

Check VIF values

vif(f4)
## Total_release_std  Transmitters_std      Training_std 
##          1.563616          1.880355          1.312943

Run models on season persistence

#----Run models on season survival----
b1 <- glm(cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ Total_release_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
b2 <- glm(cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ Transmitters_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
b3 <- glm(cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ Training_std, family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
b4 <- glm(cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ Total_release_std + Transmitters_std + Training_std , family="binomial", data=Trans_2)
b5 <- glm(cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ 1 , family="binomial", data=Trans_2)

summary(b2)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ 
##     Transmitters_std, family = "binomial", data = Trans_2)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)       -0.7830     0.1326  -5.904 3.56e-09 ***
## Transmitters_std  -0.4413     0.1199  -3.679 0.000234 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 68.194  on 26  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 52.457  on 25  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 111.82
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
summary(b4)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = cbind(Season_survive, Total_r - Season_survive) ~ 
##     Total_release_std + Transmitters_std + Training_std, family = "binomial", 
##     data = Trans_2)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)       -0.84245    0.15262  -5.520 3.39e-08 ***
## Total_release_std  0.10866    0.13192   0.824   0.4101    
## Transmitters_std  -0.32252    0.17088  -1.887   0.0591 .  
## Training_std      -0.08393    0.18314  -0.458   0.6468    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 68.194  on 26  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 51.577  on 23  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 114.94
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

Model selection of season survival models

# Run model selection
fmList<-model.sel(b1=b1, b2=b2, b3=b3, b4=b4, b5=b5)
fmList
## Model selection table 
##      (Int) Ttl_rls_std Trns_std Trnn_std df  logLik  AICc delta weight
## b2 -0.7830              -0.4413           2 -53.912 112.3  0.00  0.835
## b4 -0.8424      0.1087  -0.3225 -0.08393  4 -53.472 116.8  4.44  0.091
## b1 -0.8701      0.3190                    2 -56.431 117.4  5.04  0.067
## b3 -0.6202                      -0.35490  2 -58.812 122.1  9.80  0.006
## b5 -0.5596                                1 -61.780 125.7 13.40  0.001
## Models ranked by AICc(x)

Check residuals

Again this looks mostly ok

# Check residuals
sim_b2 <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = b2, n = 1000)
plot(sim_b2)

testDispersion(sim_b2)

## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## dispersion = 1.0807, p-value = 0.598
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
testUniformity(sim_b2)

## 
##  Exact one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
## D = 0.2187, p-value = 0.1296
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
testOutliers(sim_b2)

## 
##  DHARMa bootstrapped outlier test
## 
## data:  sim_b2
## outliers at both margin(s) = 0, observations = 27, p-value = 1
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
##  percent confidence interval:
##  0.00000000 0.01944444
## sample estimates:
## outlier frequency (expected: 0.00111111111111111 ) 
##                                                  0

Check vifs

vif(b4)
## Total_release_std  Transmitters_std      Training_std 
##          1.759938          2.114065          1.339163

Make model prediction plot

newdata <- data.frame(
  Total_release_std = seq(min(Trans_2$Total_release_std),
                          max(Trans_2$Total_release_std),
                          length = 100),
  Transmitters_std = mean(Trans_2$Transmitters_std, na.rm = TRUE),
  Training_std = mean(Trans_2$Training_std, na.rm = TRUE))

pred <- predict(f4, newdata = newdata, type = "response", se.fit = TRUE)

newdata$fit <- pred$fit
newdata$se <- pred$se.fit
newdata$lwr <- newdata$fit - 1.96 * newdata$se
newdata$upr <- newdata$fit + 1.96 * newdata$se
Trans_2$prop_survival <- Trans_2$Transloc / Trans_2$Total_r

f4plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = Trans_2,
             aes(x = Total_release_std, y = prop_survival),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(data = newdata,
            aes(x = Total_release_std, y = fit),
            size = 1.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_ribbon(data = newdata,
              aes(x = Total_release_std, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = "black") +
  labs(x = "Release group size (standardized)",
       y = "Translocation survival probability") +
  theme_classic()
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
# Make model predictions plot
newdata <- data.frame(
  Transmitters_std = seq(min(Trans_2$Transmitters_std),
                         max(Trans_2$Transmitters_std),
                         length = 100))

pred <- predict(b2, newdata = newdata, type = "response", se.fit = TRUE)

newdata$fit <- pred$fit
newdata$se <- pred$se.fit
newdata$lwr <- newdata$fit - 1.96 * newdata$se
newdata$upr <- newdata$fit + 1.96 * newdata$se
Trans_2$prop_season_survival <- Trans_2$Season_survive / Trans_2$Total_r
b2plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = Trans_2,
             aes(x = Transmitters_std, y = prop_season_survival),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(data = newdata,
            aes(x = Transmitters_std, y = fit),
            colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(data = newdata,
              aes(x = Transmitters_std, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
              fill = "black", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Transmitters (standardized)",
       y = "Predicted seasonal survival probability") +
  theme_classic()

f4plot + b2plot +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")

Plot coef estimates

## Extract coefficient estimates
f4_coefs <- tidy(f4, effects = "fixed") %>%
  mutate(model = "f4")
b2_coefs <- tidy(b2, effects = "fixed") %>%
  mutate(model = "b2")
coef_data <- bind_rows(f4_coefs, b2_coefs)

coef_data <- coef_data %>%
  rename(Variable = term) %>%
  rename(Coefficent_estimate = estimate) %>%
  mutate(model = dplyr::recode(model, "f4" = "Translocation_survival")) %>%
  mutate(model = dplyr::recode(model, "b2" = "Season_survival"))


p <- ggplot(coef_data, aes(x = Coefficent_estimate, y = Variable)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Coefficent_estimate - 1.96*std.error,
                     xmax = Coefficent_estimate + 1.96*std.error),
                 height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~model) +
  theme_bw()
p

Make survival plots

# Make survival plot

p1 <- ggplot(Trans_2,aes(x=Prop_Season_survival, y=Prop_Transloc)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white', colour = 'darkgrey')) +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, level=0.99, col='black') +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = 'white')) +
  labs(x= "Proportion of birds that survive the season", y= "Proportion of birds that survive the translocation") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black"), axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black")) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12))  

p2 <- ggplot(Trans_2,aes(x=Prop_Returns,y=Prop_Season_survival)) +   geom_smooth(method=lm, level=0.99, col='black') +
  geom_point() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white', colour = 'darkgrey')) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = 'white')) +
  labs(x= "Proportion of birds that return the following season", y= "Proportion of birds that survive the season") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black"), axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black")) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12))  

p1 + p2 +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Plot survival differences

## Plot survival differences between locations
Ave <-read.csv('Average_success2.csv', header = TRUE)
head(Ave)
##                      Value  Location Percentage    SE Upper Lower
## 1 1_Translocation survival    Birchs         74 11.06 84.76 62.64
## 2        2_Season survival    Birchs         35  6.22 41.35 28.91
## 3   3_Translocation return    Birchs          5  1.67  6.83  3.49
## 4 1_Translocation survival Melaleuca         78  5.18 83.42 73.06
## 5        2_Season survival Melaleuca         57  5.58 62.78 51.62
## 6   3_Translocation return Melaleuca          4  1.94  6.08  2.21
p = ggplot(data=Ave, aes(x=Location, y=Percentage, fill = Value)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge")
p = p + scale_color_manual(values=c("#333333", "#999999", "#CCCCCC")) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("#333333","#999999", "#CCCCCC"))
p = p + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Lower, ymax=Upper), width=.2,position=position_dodge(.9))
p = p + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white', colour = 'white'))
p = p + theme(panel.border = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line())
p = p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
p = p + labs(x= "Location", y= "Average percentage")
p = p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", size=12, margin= margin(t=0,r=20,b=0,l=0)), 
              axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size=12, margin = margin(t=20,r=0,b=20,l=0)))
p = p + theme(axis.title=element_text(size=12))
p = p + theme(legend.text=element_text(size=12))
p = p + theme(legend.title=element_text(size=12))
print(p)

Plot data source consistency

Ave <-read.csv('Consistency.csv', header = TRUE)
head(Ave)
##    Year                    Source Birds_released
## 1 1994_            ABBBS database           15.0
## 2 1994_              ZAA Studbook           17.0
## 3 1994_ OBP Recovery Team Reports           15.0
## 4 1994_     National OBP database           15.0
## 5 1994_         2014 Presentation            0.1
## 6 1996_            ABBBS database           10.0
p = ggplot(data=Ave, aes(x=Year, y=Birds_released, fill = Source)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge")
#p = p + scale_color_manual(values=c("#333333", "#999999", "#CCCCCC")) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("#333333","#999999", "#CCCCCC"))
#p = p + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Lower, ymax=Upper), width=.2,position=position_dodge(.9))
p = p + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white', colour = 'white'))
p = p + theme(panel.border = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line())
#p = p + scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:11,
#labels = paste0(c("1994", "1996", "1999", "2000", "2001", "2002", "2003"), "2004", "2005", "2007", "2008", "2009"), "year")
#p = p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
p = p + labs(x= "Year", y= "Number of birds released")
p = p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", size=15, margin= margin(t=0,r=20,b=0,l=0)), 
              axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size=15, margin = margin(t=20,r=0,b=20,l=0)))
p = p + theme(axis.title=element_text(size=12))
p = p + scale_fill_brewer(palette="YlGnBu")
p = p + theme(legend.text=element_text(size=12))
p = p + theme(legend.title=element_text(size=12))
print(p)
